---
title: "smooth_significance"
generated: true
description: "Approximate significance test for each smooth term (Wood, 2013)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.gam`](../nitrix-stats-gam.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:smooth_significance -->
```python
smooth_significance(result: GAMResult, smooths: Sequence[Smooth]) -> SmoothTest
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:smooth_significance -->
Approximate significance test for each smooth term (Wood, 2013).

The `mgcv::summary.gam` smooth-term test in its integer-rank form
(`mgcv:::testStat` with `type=1`): project the smooth's coefficients
through its design's QR factor ($R$, with $R^{\top} R =
X^{\top} X$), eigendecompose the projected Bayesian covariance
$R V R^{\top}$, and form the rank-truncated quadratic form
$T_r = \beta^{\top} V_r^{-}\,\beta$ with the rank set to the rounded
effective degrees of freedom.  $T_r$ is approximately
$\chi^2_r$ for a fixed-dispersion family, or
$F_{r,\,N - \text{edf}_{\text{total}}}$ for an estimated scale -- the
single "is `s(x)` significant, edf, p" line a developmental / brain-age
GAM analyst reads off `summary.gam`.

The test uses the forward-only `sym_eig_jacobi` eigensolver and is
**not** differentiated through.  The fractional-rank refinement (the
`mgcv` default `type=0`, which needs the weighted-chi-square CDF) is a
documented follow-up; the integer-rank test reproduces
`testStat(..., type=1)` and tracks the default closely.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:smooth_significance -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `result` | `GAMResult` | — | — | A `GAMResult` from `gam_fit`. |
| `smooths` | `Sequence[Smooth]` | — | — | The same sequence of smooth terms passed to `gam_fit`, in the same order (their `design` supplies each term's model matrix). |

**Returns** `SmoothTest`. The per-smooth test statistic, effective degrees of freedom, reference rank, and p-value, each a `(V, m)` array.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:smooth_significance -->
### References

Wood, S. N. (2013). On p-values for smooth components of an extended
generalized additive model. Biometrika, 100(1), 221-228.
https://doi.org/10.1093/biomet/ass048
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
